active_queue.assertion: The canonical RV-1 task explicitly allows an ARCHIVED disposition for each listed BEM-932 v8.3 fragment.

current_protocol.assertion: BEM-949 is the current release-audit protocol; it does not make a broad release PASS claim from historical material.

ssot_conflict_resolution.assertion: The BEM-011 conflict record materialized RV-1 as a canonical prerequisite before P7.

resolutions.rationale: Legacy BEM-932 v8.3 fragment is removed from active release-decision scope. No current canonical task or acceptance criterion depends on it outside this explicit RV-1 archival closure.
Legacy BEM-932 v8.3 fragment is retained as historical context only; it is not a substitute for a current BEM-949 evidence requirement.
No active canonical execution or release gate cites this legacy fragment; reopening it requires a new queue task with acceptance criteria and evidence path.
The grouped historical fragment is archived rather than inferred as functionally resolved. RV-1 records scope closure only.
The fragment has no independent current evidence chain. It is archived without asserting runtime, provider, policy, or release success.
The fragment is historical and non-operative under the BEM-949/BEM-011 canonical queue. Its archival closure must not be read as a Broad Release PASS.

limitations: This receipt archives legacy BEM-932 v8.3 fragments; it does not claim that those fragments were functionally remediated.
The receipt does not repair, validate, or supersede the separate DSM-1 runtime-receipt discrepancy.
P7 remains subject to every canonical prerequisite and to external Claude audit.
